_comment_repo: plpgsql template (dynamos-db migrations, dynamos-deploy, main branch).
_comment_policy: harness-perm (crates/harness-perm/src/lib.rs) supports SINGLE-ASK only: a rule fires either Allow / Ask / Deny. 'Ask twice' requires a second confirmation step. We implement it with a PreToolUse HOOK (crates/harness-core/src/hooks.rs HookEvent::PreToolUse). The hook is run AFTER the permission bucket returns Ask and the user approves; it receives the tool-call JSON on stdin and can emit {"action":"block","reason":"..."} to force a second interactive confirmation outside the permission system. Deploy the companion script to scripts/harness-dbwrite-guard.sh in the target repo (template provided in templates/settings/hooks/dbwrite_guard.sh).
_comment_shlex: Bash shlex-prefix matcher: Bash(psql -c) matches 'psql -c "INSERT ..."' because shlex tokenizes -c and the quoted SQL as two tokens. We cannot pattern-match SQL verbs inside the -c string — only the hook can inspect the full command string for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DROP/ALTER/TRUNCATE.

permissions._comment_ask: All psql/mysql invocations route through the PreToolUse hook. The hook inspects the full command, and if it contains a write verb (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DROP/ALTER/TRUNCATE/GRANT/REVOKE/CREATE/REPLACE) it BLOCKS with an explanatory reason, forcing the operator to reissue the command after manual re-confirmation (effectively a second ask).

pre_tool_use._comment: DB double-confirm: after the 'ask' bucket approval, this hook inspects the bash command and blocks psql/mysql DML/DDL so the operator must re-issue + re-approve. This is the second confirmation step. See templates/settings/hooks/dbwrite_guard.sh.
